Motion To Compel Further Responses To Document Requests And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-06-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Philip Borowsky and Christopher Hayes of Borowsky & Hayes LLP telephone number 415-896-6800 for Plaintiffs The Episcopal Church In The Diocese of California and The Episcopal Bishop of California.
Colin West and Brendan Radke of Morgan, Lewis & Bockius LLP telephone number 415-442-1121 for Defendants Episcopal Senior Communities and Senior Resources of The West.

The mattter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling. CROSS-COMPLAINANTS EPISCOPAL SENIOR COMMUNITIES and SENIOR RESOURCES OF THE WEST'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Document Requests is granted And the request For Sanctions is denied. Although there is no case directly on point, consideration of the applicable attorney ethical rules and substantive rules of corporate governance requires that as a matter of law the Bishops' representatives on defendants' boards cannot be clients of the Bishop's counsel as to any issues which the Bishop and the defendants hold adverse positions. Nor can the Bishop's representatives be clients of defendants' counsel. If they are to have counsel at all, which they may choose not to have, that counsel must be separate and independent from counsel for the Bishop and counsel for the defendants. Because there was not an attorney-client relationship as to the documents sought by this motion and those documents meet the standard of discovery relevance, the documents must be produced. The production need not occur for thirty days to allow plaintiffs, if they choose to do so, to file a writ and seek a stay of this ruling. Because the issues on this discovery motion are novel and plaintiffs and their counsel acted in good faith and had substantial justification in opposing the motion, monetary sanctions are not appropriate. Mr. West is to prepare a form of order. The Court will allow a review hearing date of June 16, 2016 on shortened time on any further issues resulting from this order. Counsel are to file the appropriate papers.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sheila Pham, CSR #13293, 415-274-9977. (302/HK)

Order To Show Cause
Off Calendar Oct-15-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. CHRISTOPHER J. HAYES, ESQ. of Borowsky & Hayes LLP, Tel No. 415-896-6800 appearing for the Plaintiffs;
Attys. STEPHEN ZOVICKIAN, ESQ. (Tel No. 415-442-1202) and BRENDAN E. RADKE, ESQ. (Tel No. 415-442-1213) of Morgan, Lewis & Bockius LLP appearing for Defendants Episcopal Senior Communities and Senior Resource of the West.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. The court denies Plaintiff's application for a preliminary injunction. Plaintiff is unlikely to prevail on its breach of charitable trust cause of action. The focus should be on whether trust assets are being used in a manner that comports with the trust purpose. See Pacific Home v. County of Los Angeles (1953) 41 Cal.2d 844, 852. Here, there is no charge that trust assets are being misused. Plaintiffs are raising issues regarding governance of the defendants, which is a distinct issue. There is insufficient proof that trust assets are being diverted or that defendants are hostile to the purpose of the trust. Plaintiffs are unlikely to prevail on the breach of Sponsorship Agreement claim. The purpose of paragraph 2.3 and Ex. 2 of that contract is to determine the governing structure where Episcopal Senior Communities affiliates with a new company. Ex. 2 specifically contemplates that Episcopal Senior Communities can change the Bishop's rights through a supermajority vote as part of the affiliation process or to change the existing governing structure. Plaintiffs merely rely on general language in the first paragraph of 2.3 and ignore the specific language of Ex. 2. Plaintiffs are unlikely to prevail on the declaratory judgment claim. Senior Resources of the West amended its bylaws in 2007. The amended bylaws allow for amendments, including to plaintiffs' prerogatives. The original bylaws required the written consent of the Bishop. That rule was based on Senior Resources of the West being a Diocesan Institution. Paragraph 18 of the Tobin indicates that it was not such an institution when the 2007 amendments occurred. In addition, the claim appears time barred. The interference claims are dubious in light of the above. Plaintiff's evidentiary objections are overruled. Plaintiff's request to not dissolve the TRO is denied. Plaintiff's request to stay the Court's order is denied. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)
